Title: Execute Main Program Routines
Preconditions: Main program is initialized and ready to execute.
Test Steps:
1. Start the main program.
2. Observe the execution of Routine 1.
3. Observe the execution of Routine 1.1 within Routine 1.
4. Observe the execution of Routine 1.2 within Routine 1.
5. Observe the execution of Routine 2.
6. Observe the execution of Routine 3.1 within Routine 2.
7. Observe the execution of Routine 3.2 within Routine 2.
8. Observe the execution of Routine 3.
9. Observe the execution of Routine 3.1 within Routine 3.
10. Observe the execution of Routine 3.2 within Routine 3.
Expected Result: All routines and sub-routines execute in the specified order without errors.

Title: Execute Main Program Routines in Reverse Order
Preconditions: Main program is initialized and ready to execute.
Test Steps:
1. Start the main program.
2. Observe the execution of Routine 3.
3. Observe the execution of Routine 3.1 within Routine 3.
4. Observe the execution of Routine 3.2 within Routine 3.
5. Observe the execution of Routine 2.
6. Observe the execution of Routine 3.1 within Routine 2.
7. Observe the execution of Routine 3.2 within Routine 2.
8. Observe the execution of Routine 1.
9. Observe the execution of Routine 1.1 within Routine 1.
10. Observe the execution of Routine 1.2 within Routine 1.
Expected Result: All routines and sub-routines execute in reverse order without errors.

Title: Create User
Test Data:
- idu: 1
- username: testuser
- mail: test@example.com
- password: testpassword
- nome: John
- cognome: Doe
- posizione: Developer
Test Steps:
1. Call getUser() method.
2. Assert that the user does not exist.
3. Call modifyUser() method to create the user.
4. Call getUser() method again.
5. Assert that the user exists with the correct details.
Expected Result: User is created successfully and can be retrieved using getUser().

Title: Modify User Details
Test Data:
- idu: 1
- username: testuser
- mail: newtest@example.com
- password: newpassword
- nome: Jane
- cognome: Smith
- posizione: Manager
Test Steps:
1. Call modifyUser() method to update user details.
2. Call getUser() method.
3. Assert that the user's details have been updated.
Expected Result: User details are modified successfully and can be verified using getUser().

Title: Successful Login
Test Steps:
1. Perform login action.
2. Verify that the information is correct.
3. Assert that access is granted.
Expected Result: User logs in successfully and access is granted.

Title: Failed Login Attempt
Test Steps:
1. Perform login action.
2. Verify that the information is incorrect.
3. Assert that an error message is displayed.
4. Retry login action.
Expected Result: User receives an error message and can retry the login process.

Title: Successful Search
Test Steps:
1. Insert input.
2. Compose query.
3. Check if the query was executed successfully.
4. Assert that the query was executed successfully.
Expected Result: Search query is executed successfully and returns expected results.

Title: Failed Search
Test Steps:
1. Insert input.
2. Compose query.
3. Check if the query was executed successfully.
4. Assert that the query was not executed successfully.
5. Display error message.
6. Retry input insertion.
Expected Result: Search query fails due to invalid input and prompts user to retry.
